fields.description:
## Why
The declarative descriptors + the generic chunker/router are the engine of the gate; production prompts live
in the workflow-engine prompt library (not the experiment JSON).

## What
- criteria_v8 descriptors -> production prompt library (resolve_prompt / prompts-as-files).
- Generic orchestrator: applies_at level/type routing; facet chunker (base_chunk x size_factor); exec-tier
  dispatch (single-turn / 2-step / agent); overlay routing (deterministic + LLM-router).

## Scope
- NO TRUNCATION BY DESIGN. A cap on the reviewed artifact INVALIDATES the review (it reviews a plan that is
  not the one in rebar), so the reviewed plan + children are NEVER truncated — no char cap anywhere. The
  orchestrator reads the compiled `rebar show` description (not a capped/event view). Size is handled by the
  mechanisms below; a cap is never one of them.
- SIZE-HANDLING ORDER (when full content would exceed the model context — distinct from rubric-chunking and
  the LLM-call budget): (1) FEED WHOLE — normal tickets fit a large window. (2) CONTEXT-WINDOW ESCALATION —
  before any decomposition, escalate to a LARGER-CONTEXT model: estimate content tokens and pick the smallest
  model on a configured ladder whose window fits (with headroom); a context-limit API error escalates to the
  next-larger model. (3) STRUCTURAL DECOMPOSITION — review each child/ticket at its own level + the epic's own
  ACs, aggregate (the proportionate-scrutiny model). (4) SECTION-CHUNK a single giant ticket by AC/section with
  FULL coverage (never drop the tail). (5) INDETERMINATE for any uncovered part — never silently certify a
  partial review.
- WHOLE-SET criteria (G3 child-coverage, G4 child-consistency) inherently need the parent + ALL children
  together — they cannot be evaluated child-in-isolation (the question is 'do the children completely and
  accurately represent the parent'). Normal case: fed whole. Over-budget: MAP-REDUCE over the children — process
  them in batches (each child WHOLE, never truncated) and aggregate DETERMINISTICALLY: G3 = which parent ACs each
  batch covers → union → the uncovered set; G4 = the 7 interaction modes within each batch and across batch
  boundaries (cluster by shared files/state). Accuracy (a child mis-representing a parent AC) needs the child
  BODY, so batches carry full bodies. INDETERMINATE only if even batched coverage cannot complete.
- MISSING-INFO / ABSENCE false-positives (the partial-view risk): the default chunking splits the RUBRIC
  (criteria) across calls while feeding the FULL plan to EACH — so every finder keeps the whole picture and
  CANNOT raise a missing-info FP from a partial view (chunk the RUBRIC, never the CONTENT). Content is decomposed
  ONLY when it exceeds context; when it is, an absence / 'missing X' finding from a content-slice is a CANDIDATE
  ONLY — Pass-2 must confirm X is absent from the COMPLETE artifact (full plan + children, via retrieval) before
  it stands. Absence findings carry a higher verification bar; the finder may work on a view, but the VERIFIER
  must have whole-artifact access. (Huge SUPPORTING context — e.g. the ISF linked session log — uses
  DETERMINISTIC pre-retrieval of decision-bearing sections with the selection RECORDED as coverage; lossy but
  explicit, and only for supporting context, never the primary plan.)
- Coverage (which model/window, any escalation/decomposition/INDETERMINATE) is RECORDED on the attestation so a
  partial/over-budget review is visible, not hidden.
- Built ON the workflow engine; the exact SHAPE (gate-as-workflow vs bespoke; dynamic fan-out handling)
  depends on the `6f2d` (soupy-spool-revel) brainstorm this epic depends_on. Reuse rebar.llm extension points.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Registry loads from the prompt library; the coverage guard passes.
- [ ] Orchestrator filters via applies_at, packs by facet, dispatches by exec tier.
- [ ] No criterion greps the codebase except the code-grounding set (E4/G1G2/A1/G6).
- [ ] The orchestrator reviews the FULL plan + children — NO TRUNCATION BY DESIGN (no char cap on the reviewed artifact). Over-budget content is handled by context-window model escalation → structural decomposition / section-chunk-with-full-coverage → INDETERMINATE, never a cap; coverage recorded.
- [ ] Whole-set criteria (G3/G4) get parent + ALL children together (map-reduce over children with full bodies + deterministic aggregation when over-budget); absence/missing-info findings from any content-slice are verified against the COMPLETE artifact before they stand (no partial-view FPs).
- [ ] A context-window escalation mechanism: estimate tokens / catch context-limit errors → escalate up a configured model-by-window ladder before decomposing.
